text: Moving from the quiet, open, almost clinical emptiness of the first space to the vibrant, intellectually charged exhibition of the second, I feel a shift. In the lobby or corridor, I was contemplative, aware of the weight of emptiness and potential. Amidst the R&D display, I sense curiosity and engagement—a silent invitation to question and connect, both with the ideas presented and the people who shaped them. This juxtaposition of isolation and stimulation highlights how space can mold my thoughts, either freeing them or focusing them inward.
